Paragraph

Note that when \(f\) is continuous on the closed rectangle \(R\text{,}\) we achieve (5.2.2) by constructing a partition \(\cP:=\{R_{\alpha}\}\) of \(R\) such that \(\osc(f, R_{\alpha}) < \epsilon/\vert R\vert\) for all \(R_{\alpha} \in \cP\text{.}\) We can also achieve (5.2.2) if we can construct a partition \(\cP:=\{R_{\alpha}\}\) of \(R\) such that the sum of volume of those rectangles \(R_{\alpha}\text{,}\) for which \(\osc(f, R_{\alpha})\ge \epsilon/(2\vert R\vert)\text{,}\) can be made smaller than \(\epsilon/(2 \osc(f, R))\) , as the factors \((f, R_{\alpha})\) have a uniform upper bound \(\osc(f, R)\) for a given bounded function \(f\) on \(R\text{.}\) We will exploit some properties of \(\osc(f)(\bx)\) for this.
in-context
